keyTerms.Crystal Palace: Joseph Paxton's 1851 exhibition hall of glass and iron, symbolizing Victorian industrial confidence and progress
keyTerms.East End slums: The overcrowded, unsanitary working-class districts of Victorian London documented by social reformers and novelists
keyTerms.Factory Acts: A series of nineteenth-century laws that progressively limited working hours and child labor in British industry
keyTerms.Charles Dickens: Victorian novelist whose works — Oliver Twist, A Christmas Carol, Bleak House — exposed the era's social extremes
keyTerms.indoor plumbing: The gradual introduction of piped water and flush toilets to middle-class homes, transforming Victorian domestic life
